items.title: Thousands rally in Northern Ireland to condemn anti-immigration rioting
items.summary_auto: Thousands of demonstrators gathered in Belfast on Saturday to denounce anti-immigrant rioters who set fire to homes and cars earlier in the week after a brutal stabbing blamed on an asylum seeker.

items.title: World Cup kicks off in Vancouver. Here's what you need to know
items.summary_auto: Seven soccer matches at Vancouver's B.C. Place expected to be attended by 350,000 fans.

items.title: Ghana slams Canada’s decision to deny World Cup visa for Partey, who faces rape charges
items.summary_auto: Ghana’s government has criticized Canada’s “extremely unfair” decision to deny midfielder Thomas Partey a visa for his team’s World Cup opener while he awaits trial on rape charges, and it threatened legal action in a bid to overturn the refusal.

items.title: Canada vows to restrict social media for kids under 16. Teens say they'll 'always find a way'
Anthropic says it has taken its latest AI models offline to comply with U.S. directive
items.summary_auto: AI giant Anthropic said on Friday it has taken its latest artificial intelligence models, known as Fable 5 and Mythos 5, offline to comply with a directive from the Trump administration to prevent their use by foreign nationals.

items.title: Montreal mayor urges calm, vows to uncover truth after police unit suspected of racist behaviour
Officials signal U.S. and Iran expected to sign initial deal, but timeline uncertain
items.summary_auto: Iran said on Saturday it could sign the framework for a peace deal with the United States in the coming days, but dismissed a suggestion by mediator Pakistan that it would be signed within the next 24 hours.

items.title: Several people killed, injured after crash near Kitchener-Waterloo, Ont., police say
items.summary_auto: Multiple people were killed, and several others were injured in a two-vehicle collision in rural Ontario.

items.title: Residents in Brooks, Alta., seek info on the data centre megaproject planned right next door
items.summary_auto: A substantial but largely confidential plan to build a huge data centre near Brooks, Alta., has led local officials to update bylaws and launch an education program for residents about the benefits, drawbacks and potential mitigation strategies of hosting such large-scale industry.

items.title: Brooks residents seek info on the data centre megaproject planned right next door
items.summary_auto: A substantial but largely confidential plan to build a huge data centre near Brooks, Alta., has led local officials to update bylaws and launch an education program for residents about the benefits, drawbacks and potential mitigation strategies of hosting such large-scale industry.

items.title: AI cameras being piloted to detect wildfires in Kananaskis
items.summary_auto: AltaLink is testing AI-enabled wildfire cameras in Kananaskis that can automatically detect smoke and alert staff to potential fire starts. AI wildfire detection cameras are being increasingly being rolled out across western North America as communities look for new ways to detect wildfires quicker.

items.title: New Canada Post stamp celebrates Halifax 2SLGBTQ+ history
items.summary_auto: Halifax’s historic lesbian and gay club, The Turret, is being honoured through Canada Post’s Places of Pride stamp series.

items.title: Trump's White House UFC headliner accused of assault, forced sex, stalking
Black women in Canada are facing a mental health crisis. This clinic is trying to help
items.summary_auto: A recent report reveals that mental health is the most commonly diagnosed chronic condition among Black women in Canada. Clinicians, researchers and patients say that’s not surprising, pointing to the ongoing effects of anti-Black racism and misogyny that they face in everyday life.

items.title: Red flag or red herring? The numbers behind assisted dying in the Netherlands
items.summary_auto: Assisted deaths among people with mental disorders in the Netherlands have surged in recent years. Some Dutch psychiatrists say that's a red flag for Canada, but not everyone agrees.

items.title: Ottawa has struggled to increase grocery competition. Will the new food strategy help?
items.summary_auto: The federal government has pledged $3 billion which it says will add more food terminals in Canada, help the Competition Bureau sniff out and penalize anticompetitive behaviour, and help Canada produce more of its own food. Independent grocers say it's a step in the right direction when it comes to helping them compete, but won't topple the dominance of the country's major grocers.

items.title: Some provinces want more say in appointing judges. What does Canada's chief justice think?
items.summary_auto: Several provinces are calling for a constitutional change to give themselves more say in how federal judges are appointed at the provincial level. What does Chief Justice Richard Wagner make of this ongoing political debate over Canada's judicial system? CBC's The House spoke with him earlier this week to get his thoughts.

items.title: From riots to 'regularization,' U.K. and Spain show opposing attitudes to migrants
items.summary_auto: The Pope’s visit this week to Spain’s Canary Islands, where he met with migrants and their advocates, underscores the polarized debate in Europe over immigration. As Spain welcomes half a million illegal migrants, the streets of British cities are burning as forces on the political right push to keep them out.

items.title: Amid excitement, FIFA World Cup sets off worries over disruptions and safety in host cities
items.summary_auto: Concerns from traffic to littering and over-policing have Canadians in host cities worried about the FIFA World Cup and its effects on local communities.

items.title: Out-of-this-world medical tech could boost health care on Earth, researchers say
items.summary_auto: The shift toward deep space exploration is set to bring new developments for Earth’s health-care systems, researchers say, including innovations such as portable medical technology and robotic care.

items.title: Tupac is being digitally resurrected again, this time in a video game. Not everyone likes it
items.summary_auto: Gamers and influencers watching Summer Game Fest mostly expressed shock and confusion at the inclusion of the influential American rapper and political activist who died in 1996.

items.title: Saturday night showdown: Australia vs. Turkey opens FIFA World Cup in Vancouver
items.summary_auto: Fan support for both nations is expected to be strong inside B.C. Place come game time.

items.title: Longtime, pun-loving movie critic Gene Shalit dead at age 100
items.summary_auto: Pun-spouting ​movie critic Gene Shalit, a fixture on NBC's Today show for decades in his bow ties and extravagantly bushy hair and moustache, died on Friday at the age of 100, NBC News reported, citing a family statement shared with the network.

items.title: Iran's military has been degraded. What's left?
items.summary_auto: Earlier this week, the U.S. launched two nights of airstrikes on Iran. Those attacks seemed to suggest that the U.S. believes the regime, despite weeks of coming under constant bombardment before the April ceasefire, still retains a significant cache of weapons that need to be taken out.

items.title: Trump’s name is gone from Kennedy Center’s facade, says top official at the arts venue
items.summary_auto: Workers began removing U.S. President Donald Trump's name from the facade of the Kennedy Center early Saturday, hours after a court-ordered Friday deadline to remove references to Trump from the building and other aspects of the iconic performing arts venue's operations. His name is now reportedly gone.

items.title: SpaceX: The IPO where the math doesn't matter | About That
items.summary_auto: SpaceX became a publicly traded company, making founder Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire. Andrew Chang takes a closer look at all the numbers to explore what investors are betting on, and breaks down the risks that come with such a future-facing company. (Photo credits: The Canadian Press, Reuters, Adobe Stock and Getty Images)
